mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions triggers risk-off positioning as investors price in higher recession probabilities, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer credit growth signals a structural shift in aggregate demand composition, where rising credit coupled with flat housing indicates service-sector dominance that sustains equity cash flows and drives positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random walk benchmarks over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production following a supply chain disruption restores corporate margin expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds coincide with negative equity returns due to input cost inflation, or the signal fails to predict returns during supply-side stagflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing positive forward returns over a 5-day horizon as institutional flows chase momentum in a growing economy.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth accelerates while equity valuations contract due to multiple compression, or the rolling correlation between GDP surprises and SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital suppresses equity risk premiums and forces portfolio rebalancing away from duration-sensitive assets, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite rates above 5.0% driven by productivity shocks, or the threshold signal produces a positive Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and stable unemployment rates confirm a healthy labor market without wage-push inflation, supporting corporate profitability and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when employment growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly rising consumer credit balances indicate leveraged consumption that precedes a demand shock, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as investors front-run the impending earnings downgrade cycle.
failure_condition: Consumer credit expansion coincides with sustained equity rallies due to wage growth absorption, or the signal yields a negative predictive relationship in post-2015 data.
